summary: The hormone primarily inhibited directly at a hypothalamic level is Prolactin due to continuous dopaminergic input suppressing it; whereas there isn't strong evidence indicating similar direct inhibition over Growth Hormone production via such mechanisms involving specific neurotransmitters within the brain region itself. Agent1 identified two main hormones—Prolactin and Growth Hormone—and highlighted how only one has clear documented hypthahlamic suppression mediated mainly by dopamine influencing its output
